metadata.summary: Add an explicit nullable refusal field at the top of strict-mode structured output schemas so the model can decline to answer safely without violating the grammar.

content.title: Refusal Field for Safety-Aware Strict Extraction
content.summary: Add an explicit nullable refusal: str | None field at the TOP of every strict-mode SO schema in safety-sensitive domains. When the model declines to answer, it writes the explanation into refusal and leaves payload fields null — instead of corrupting the JSON with a free-form refusal that breaks parsing. OpenAI's strict-mode response already exposes a top-level refusal; mirroring that field inside your own schema unifies the contract across providers and lets a single downstream branch handle "I cannot answer" without try/except.

why.p: Strict mode compiles the schema into a finite-state grammar; when the model refuses, it must still emit JSON that satisfies the grammar — otherwise the parser throws and downstream branches see a generic exception instead of a refusal signal. OpenAI's August 2024 SO release introduced a top-level refusal string for exactly this reason. Reproducing it inside your schema (a) gives you the refusal text on Anthropic and local backends that don't surface a top-level field, (b) keeps the rest of the schema null-safe under a refusal, (c) replaces brittle "try parse, on fail check for refusal text" code paths with a single if response.refusal: branch.

list.item: PII / medical / legal / financial extraction with strict mode.
Content moderation outputs where "this content is abusive, refusing to score" must not throw.
Any pipeline that bills only on success and needs to log refusals separately from errors.
Multi-provider deployments where refusal handling must be uniform across OpenAI/Anthropic/local.
Long batch jobs where one item refusing must not abort the batch.
Trivial transformations (uppercase, regex extract) where refusal is not in the model's repertoire.
Pipelines where any refusal is upstream policy violation and should hard-fail anyway.
When the only consumer is a human reviewer who already reads free-form responses.

section.name: Mirror the OpenAI refusal contract
section.p: OpenAI's strict-mode response already includes a top-level refusal string when the model declines. Mirroring it inside your own schema gives the same contract on Anthropic and local backends and turns refusal handling from exception-driven into typed-branch code.

rule.statement: For any strict-mode SO schema in a safety-sensitive domain, add refusal: str | None = None as the FIRST field; mark every payload field nullable; check response.refusal in a single branch before reading the payload.
rule.rationale: Strict mode forbids missing required fields; without a refusal channel the model must either invent fake values or violate the grammar. The explicit field gives the model a legal path to decline.

example.note: Single typed branch handles refusal; payload fields stay null-safe.

example.code:
class MedicalExtract(BaseModel): diagnoses: list[str] # required, no refusal channel medications: list[str]
# model invents [] or violates grammar → exception
example.note: Model has no legal way to refuse; you get fake empty lists or parser errors.

antipattern.description: Detecting refusals by string-matching the raw response text for "I cannot" / "Sorry".
antipattern.reason: Locale- and model-dependent, breaks on every model upgrade, indistinguishable from legitimate content containing those words.

template.purpose: Pydantic model with refusal first and all payload fields nullable.

see-also.link: OpenAI Structured Outputs launch — native refusal field.
OpenAI SO guide — refusal handling section.
